title: AuthGuard, Routing, and Authorization
description: Review Angular route protection, route configuration, and when to use guards for authorization.
checklist: Explain how AuthGuard uses CanActivate for protected routes
Show where to register guards in route definitions
Describe route parameter syntax and child routes
Discuss role-based or permission-based route decisions
Explain why auth logic belongs in a guard instead of a component
answerHtml:
Sub QuestionAnswerMore ExplanationExampleBenefit
What is AuthGuard?Route guard implementing CanActivateChecks auth state before route activation{ path: 'secure', canActivate: [AuthGuard] }Protects routes centrally
Where do you configure routes?In RouterModule.forRoot() or forChild()Defines path, component, children, and guardsconst routes = [{ path: 'admin', component: AdminComponent }];Central route configuration
How do you handle route params?Use :id in the path and ActivatedRouteSupports dynamic segments and nested routes{ path: 'users/:id', component: UserDetailComponent }Allows reusable route templates
How does authorization differ from authentication?Auth = identity, authorization = allowed actionsUsers can be authenticated but still restrictedAuthGuard checks roles or permissionsSafer application access control
Why keep auth logic in a guard?Separates security from UI componentsComponents stay focused on renderingGuard returns true/false or redirectsCleaner, maintainable routing security
